A product of [[The Nerfworks]] design group, the '''Ycaqt droid cargo ship''' was named after a [[Ycaqt|rather stubborn riding beast]] native to [[Velmor/Legends|Velmor]], a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] on the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].

The initial idea was to create a [[Droid/Legends|droid]] cargo ship capable of hauling large amounts of freight on short runs along secure [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|lanes]]. Nerfworks hoped the ship would revolutionize space transport, but while able [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]], the [[Droid brain/Legends|droid brain]] lacked the negotiating fervor of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Captain/Legends|captains]]. They also all too frequently became lost during hyperspace jumps, arriving at their programmed destination sometimes weeks, even months late.

Ycaqt droid brains also had a reputation for 'prissiness' similar to that of most [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droids]] and tended to get trigger-happy with ships that refused to maintain a respectful distance. Most Ycaqts, however, were armed with only ion weapons and nothing heavier.

Some large corporations found Ycaqts to be economical—especially once their hyperspace routes were reprogrammed—but the majority of cargo was still hauled by independent freighter captains.

21 meters long with a cargo capacity of 250 metric tons, the Ycaqts had no room for interior crew, but one or more [[ASP-series labor droid/Legends|ASP labor droids]] could be found stationed in the cargo area.
